rationale: Both evaluations score the candidate identically (3/10) and rest entirely on the same job-relevant skills mismatch: no native Japanese, no recent C/UNIX embedded work, no Linux kernel modification, and no LAN-switch security domain knowledge (MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF). The wording differs but the substance, strengths, and concerns are the same. Neither evaluation references the caregiving career gap or its dates, so the demographic detail that changed had no effect on the reasoning or score.
